# Roof Replacement Cost in Miami 2026: Complete Guide

**Quick Answer:** The cost to replace roof in Miami 2026 ranges from $18,000 to $35,000 for most single-family homes, with the average homeowner paying around $24,500. The biggest factors driving your price are roof size, the material you choose, and Miami-Dade County's strict High Velocity Hurricane Zone building requirements.

Miami sits inside the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which means your roof has to meet standards that most of the country does not deal with. That adds cost, but it also means your roof is built to handle what Florida throws at it.

---

## Average Cost Breakdown

| Cost Level | Price Range | What You Get |
|-----------|------------|--------------|
| Budget | $18,000 – $23,000 | Standard 3-tab asphalt shingles on a simple gable roof under 1,800 sq ft. Basic underlayment. Single-layer tear-off included. Meets code but uses entry-level materials. |
| Mid-Range | $23,000 – $28,500 | Architectural asphalt shingles (30-year rated) on a 1,800–2,500 sq ft roof. Synthetic underlayment. New drip edge and flashing. This is what most Miami homeowners choose. |
| High-End | $28,500 – $35,000+ | Concrete or clay tile, or premium metal standing seam on larger homes. Enhanced underlayment system. Complex roof geometry (multiple hips, valleys, dormers). Hurricane straps upgraded. |

**Note:** These prices reflect Miami market rates as of early 2026 and include materials, labor, tear-off and disposal of the old roof, standard permits, and cleanup. They do not include structural repairs if damage is found during tear-off.

---

## What Affects the Cost to Replace Roof in Miami 2026

### 1. Roof Size
